sentence.text: CR
The Native Tribes of the US have lived here since before the end of the last ice age.
Groups settled many parts of the land and each group had their own way of life.
They became the Tribes of the Great Plains.
The plains stretch straight through the center of the country.
They cross rivers and deserts.
In some parts, a person can see for hundreds of miles over flat lands.
Large stretches of this land used to be covered by high, thick grasses.
CR
CR
The grass fed the buffulo that were the center of the lives of the Great Plains Tribes.
The animals gave them meat, and much more.
Buffalo were central to their spirit life as well.
The horse played an important part in the life of the Tribes too.
The horse gave them a new way to travel.
It allowed them to follow and hunt the buffalo more safely.
CR
The coming of white settlers to the Great Plains changed things.
It saw the end of the horse and buffalo life of the Tribes.
Settlers did not want buffalo to destroy their crops and killed many of them.
The Native Tribes struggled with this threat to their way of life and fought it.
This way of life came to an end when they were forced to live on limited lands.
